top_signals.title: Warsh Faces First Big Test as Fed Chair
top_signals.summary:
Federal Reserve policymakers are expected to hold interest rates steady on Wednesday, posing an early test for new chairman Kevin Warsh as rising inflation erodes households’ purchasing power and President Donald Trump continues to press for lower borrowing costs.  
Robert Kaplan, Vice Chairman at Goldman Sachs, discusses what to expect from Kevin Warsh's first meeting as Fed chair and his outlook for markets after the FOMC decision. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Bulgaria to Oppose EU Sanctions Against Russian Patriarch Kirill
top_signals.summary: Bulgaria’s new government will oppose sanctions against the head of the Russian Orthodox Church included in the latest European Union package of measures targeting Moscow, the country’s foreign minister said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
top_signals.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Billions in SpaceX Trades Jolt ETFs Including Cathie Wood’s ARKK
top_signals.summary: A series of unusual multibillion-dollar flows has rippled through the exchange-traded fund market in the past week, suggesting some major investors used a contentious method to get exposure to the initial public offering of SpaceX. At least one fund manager placed temporary restrictions on its product in a bid to curb the practice.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Great British Summer Savings: Major businesses pass on VAT cut to families on days out
top_signals.summary: Some of the UK’s most prominent leisure and hospitality businesses have committed to passing on summer savings to families as they met the Chancellor today – ahead of the government’s Great British Summer Savings scheme going live.
